name: Burbáguena - Luco de Jiloca Trail (PR-TE 39)
name_original: Burbáguena - Luco de Jiloca – ruta circular (PR-TE 39)
description: Hike the difficult 11.8-mile Burbáguena - Luco de Jiloca Trail (PR-TE 39) through pine forests, cherry orchards, and historic towns.
region_intro: Hiking around Calamocha offers diverse landscapes, transitioning from the plains of the Jiloca valley to the mountainous areas of the Iberian System. The region features significant natural elements such as the Jiloca River, the Pancrudo River Gorge with its geological formations and waterfalls, and unique karst reliefs like the Aguallueve de Anento. These varied terrains provide a range of hiking experiences across plains, riverbanks, and mountain foothills. Best hiking trails around Calamocha The most popular hiking route is Aguallueve and Arguilay Circular Trail, a 7.1 miles (11.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 10 minutes to complete. This route explores the unique Aguallueve spring, where water continuously falls in drops, creating moss-covered walls. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pancrudo River Gorge Trail, a moderate 4.7 miles (7.6 km) path. This trail features geological formations, towering cliffs, and the Molino waterfall within the Pancrudo River Gorge. Local hikers also love the Río Jiloca and Camino de la Azucarera Trail, an 11.4 miles (18.3 km) trail leading through river landscapes and past the Lechago Reservoir, often completed in about 4 hours 45 minutes. Hiking around Calamocha is defined by the Jiloca River valley, the Iberian System mountains, and impressive geological formations. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Calamocha are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 100 hikers have used komoot to explore Calamocha's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there around Calamocha?
A: There are over 10 hiking trails available around Calamocha, offering a diverse range of experiences. These routes cater to various fitness levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult options.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking trails in Calamocha?
A: Yes, Calamocha offers several easy trails suitable for families. While specific easy routes are not detailed here, the region's varied terrain includes gentle paths along the Jiloca River valley, which are generally suitable for a relaxed outing. Always check the route's difficulty before heading out.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in the Calamocha area?
A: For those seeking a challenge, Calamocha has several difficult routes. One notable option is the &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981421202/burbaguena-luco-de-jiloca-trail-pr-te-39" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Burbáguena - Luco de Jiloca Trail (PR-TE 39)&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt;, an 11.8-mile (18.9 km) path that takes you through pine forests and cherry orchards, offering a more strenuous experience.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Calamocha?
A: Yes, there are excellent circular routes available. A popular choice is the &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974320377/aguallueve-and-arguilay-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguallueve and Arguilay Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt;, a moderate 7.1-mile (11.4 km) loop that explores the unique Aguallueve spring and a historic castle.

Q: Are there any hikes with waterfalls or unique water features in Calamocha?
A: Absolutely! The region is known for its impressive water features. You can explore the &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981563439/pancrudo-river-gorge-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pancrudo River Gorge Trail&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt;, which features the beautiful Molino waterfall. Additionally, the &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974320377/aguallueve-and-arguilay-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguallueve and Arguilay Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt; leads to the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5088573" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguallueve de Anento&lt;/a&gt;, a spring where water continuously falls in drops, creating spectacular moss-covered walls.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking around Calamocha?
A: Calamocha offers incredibly diverse landscapes. You'll find trails along the extensive plains of the Jiloca River valley, through the dramatic Pancrudo River Gorge with its towering cliffs, and into the mountainous areas of the Iberian System. The region also features unique geological formations and karst reliefs.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest along the hiking trails?
A: Yes, Calamocha is rich in history. The area is part of the famous Camino del Cid tourist route, with a significant stop at El Poyo del Cid. You might also encounter historic towns, castles, and structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5182236" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman bridge of Luco de Jiloca&lt;/a&gt; on some routes, blending natural beauty with cultural heritage.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Calamocha?
A: The hiking routes in Calamocha are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 10 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse terrain, from river landscapes to geological wonders, and the well-maintained paths that allow for varied experiences.

Q: Are there any trails that follow rivers or explore river valleys?
A: Yes, the Jiloca River is a prominent feature of the region. The &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981575727/rio-jiloca-and-camino-de-la-azucarera-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Jiloca and Camino de la Azucarera Trail&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt; is an 11.4-mile (18.3 km) route that specifically explores the beautiful river landscapes and passes by the Lechago Reservoir.

Q: Can I combine hiking with exploring nearby towns or attractions?
A: Absolutely. Many trails are close to or pass through charming settlements. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5088573" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguallueve de Anento&lt;/a&gt; is near the village of Anento, and you can visit towns like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5058067" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calamocha&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5058102" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monreal del Campo&lt;/a&gt;, which offer local amenities and a glimpse into the regional culture.

Q: Are there any specific geological formations to look out for on the trails?
A: Calamocha is known for its significant geological importance. Besides the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5088573" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguallueve de Anento&lt;/a&gt;, you'll encounter impressive karst reliefs and limestone formations, especially in areas like the Pancrudo River Gorge. The 'paraje de los tollos' also offers geological formations resulting from erosion, providing stunning views of the Jiloca and Pancrudo valleys.
tips: Beautiful and quiet town in the interior of Teruel that is located on the banks of the Jiloca River. Within its heritage is the picturesque Mudejar-style church and its iconic medieval bridge with three arches that crosses the river.

Settled on the banks of the Jiloca River, this beautiful and quiet town in Turol is worth visiting. There are records of it in documents dating from the year 1156 at that time called "Burbaca" and it is still unknown for sure if its origin is Arab or pre-Roman. From here you can start several hiking trails, some towards the interior of Teruel like the one that runs through Luco de Jiloca, and others towards the interior of Zaragoza, a neighboring province.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike the Burbáguena - Luco de Jiloca Trail (PR-TE 39)?
faqs.answer: This circular hike typically takes around 5 hours and 11 minutes to complete. The route covers approximately 18.9 kilometers (11.7 miles).

faqs.question: Where does the Burbáguena - Luco de Jiloca Trail (PR-TE 39) start, and is there parking?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route connecting the towns of Burbáguena and Luco de Jiloca. You can start in either town. Parking is generally available in these villages, which serve as natural trailheads for the route.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain and scenery can I expect on this trail?
faqs.answer: You'll experience a diverse landscape, ascending from the Jiloca valley to the Campo de Romanos. The terrain includes dense reforested pine forests, especially in the Val de la Cueva ravine, and later opens up to fertile agricultural plains and riverine groves along the Jiloca River. Look out for cherry orchards, holm oaks, gall oaks, and centuries-old "chopo cabecero" (headed poplar) trees.

faqs.question: How difficult is the Burbáguena - Luco de Jiloca Trail (PR-TE 39)?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as difficult. It involves an accumulated elevation gain of about 306 meters (1,004 feet) and a similar descent. The route includes an 8 km ascent from Luco de Jiloca, reaching a maximum elevation of 1,092 meters at Alto de las Cumbres, making it a challenging but rewarding hike.

faqs.question: What historical or natural features can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail follows ancient paths, some associated with the tradition of Saints Vincent and Valerius. You'll see extensive reforested pine forests with unique gabion walls, and glimpses into the agricultural traditions of the Jiloca Valley. In Burbáguena, you can find a medieval castle and noble houses, while Luco de Jiloca features a Roman bridge.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The Jiloca region experiences varied seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant hiking conditions with milder temperatures and beautiful natural colors. Summer can be hot, especially in the open agricultural areas, while winter may bring colder temperatures and potentially snow at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Is the Burbáguena - Luco de Jiloca Trail (PR-TE 39) dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas, near livestock, or in sections shared with other users. Please ensure you clean up after your pet to maintain the trail's cleanliness.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Burbáguena - Luco de Jiloca Trail (PR-TE 39). It is a public access route.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other long-distance routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other significant long-distance trails. You'll find parts of the Camino del Cid (Senderista) and the Vera Cruz along the way, offering a chance to experience a piece of these historic paths.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities or places to eat near the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail connects the towns of Burbáguena and Luco de Jiloca. Both villages offer basic amenities, which may include small shops, cafes, or restaurants where you can refuel before or after your hike.

faqs.question: Is the Burbáguena - Luco de Jiloca Trail suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its difficult rating, length of nearly 19 km, and significant elevation changes, this trail is generally not recommended for beginners or very young children. It's best suited for experienced hikers with a good level of fitness.
